result:
All changes are complete and all existing tests pass. Here's a summary of the fix:

## Root Cause

All four naming rules used ASCII-only regex character classes (`[a-z]`, `[A-Z]`, `[a-zA-Z]`) to validate identifiers. These patterns reject valid Unicode letters like Nordic characters (å, ä, ö, æ, ø), even though Kotlin allows any Unicode letter in identifiers.

## Changes Made

### 1. `PackageNameRule.kt` (standard ruleset)
- `[a-z_]` → `[\\p{Ll}_]` (Unicode lowercase letter or underscore)
- `[a-zA-Z\\d_]` → `[\\p{L}\\d_]` (Unicode letter, digit, or underscore)

### 2. `ClassNamingRule.kt` (experimental ruleset)
- `[A-Z]` → `[\\p{Lu}]` (Unicode uppercase letter)
- `[A-Za-z\\d]` → `[\\p{L}\\d]` (Unicode letter or digit)

### 3. `FunctionNamingRule.kt` (experimental ruleset)
- `[a-z]` → `[\\p{Ll}]` (Unicode lowercase letter)
- `[A-Za-z\\d]` → `[\\p{L}\\d]` (Unicode letter or digit)
- Same changes applied to test function name regex

### 4. `PropertyNamingRule.kt` (experimental ruleset)
- `LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP`: `[a-z]` → `[\\p{Ll}]`, `[a-zA-Z]` → `[\\p{L}]`
- `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP`: `[A-Z]` → `[\\p{Lu}]`
- `BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP`: `[a-z]` → `[\\p{Ll}]`, `[a-zA-Z]` → `[\\p{L}]`

The Unicode property classes used:
- `\p{Ll}` — Unicode lowercase letter (includes å, ä, ö, æ, ø, etc.)
- `\p{Lu}` — Unicode uppercase letter (includes Å, Ä, Ö, Æ, Ø, etc.)
- `\p{L}` — any Unicode letter (both cases)
